rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 11, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF SELMA OZER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES VS. SHARON CASSIDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 11, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES' Motion For Reconsideration. This motion will be heard at 9:00 am in Department 302 by Judge Kahn. The motion is granted. The prior order is vacated and the default and default judgment are reinstated. The prior order stated that relief was granted per CCP 437(d), which refers to void judgments. Although the prior order did not state whether the default and default judgment were void on their face or facially valid but void for lack of service, the prior order did state that defendant "presented sufficient evidence to show that she was not properly served." This language clearly shows that I determined that the default and default judgment were void for lack of service because a facially void order is determined solely by looking at the proof of service and does not consider the assertedly served party's evidence. A motion to set aside a default and default judgment based on the grounds that they are facially valid but void for lack of service must be filed within two years of the entry of the default and default judgment. Here, the default judgment was entered on May 3, 2010, but the motion to set aside was not filed until July 6, 2012, which makes the motion untimely. Reconsideration is warranted under CCP 1008 because neither at the hearing nor in the parties' papers was any distinction made between a facially void judgment and a facially valid judgment which is void due to improper service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IGX GROUP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. 490 POST STREET LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 11, 2013, Line 27, DEFENDANTs 490 POST STREET LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY, UNIVERSITY CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., A CALIFORNIA, BROADMOOR CAPITAL CORPORATION's mOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. There are triable issues of fact whether Defendants failed to disclose material facts during the sale, and summary judgment is denied. (See evidence cited by Plaintiffs in response to UMF 8, 17-19, 21, 25, 27, 29, 33, 34, 36-38, 42, 45, 47, 48, 50-52, 57-58.) 1. Group is a proper party to this action if defendants' misrepresentations/concealment induced it to occupy suite 1700. Defendants fail to shift the burden on this issue. Group was part of the negotiations for the purchase of the property and agreed to occupy the premises based on Defendants' alleged misrepresentations and concealments. Koonce was the negotiator for both Group and Properties (Koonce Decl. paras. 2-9.) 2. Summary adjudication is denied as to UCM and Broadmoor as not being proper parties. The moving papers and separate statement do not specifically raise an argument on behalf of Broadmoor. Defendants fails to distinguish between tort and contract causes of action. UCM and Broadmoor can be liable for their tortious conduct. 3. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether plaintiffs exercised due diligence prior to purchasing/occupying the property. The exposed wires on the pull station, dead bolt locks, and a condominium map that did not refer to the egress all create a triable issue of fact whether Plaintiffs should have discovered the encumbrance. (Koonce Decl. paras. 6-10.) For all of the reasons set forth above, Defendants have failed to show that Plaintiffs cannot establish the individual causes of action and summary adjudication is denied as to each of them. Further, there is a triable issue of fact as to whether Mr. Alsaidi's knowledge of the pull station and fire exit through Suite 1700 is imputed to defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JESSE MORALES et al VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 11, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF JESSE MORALES, KATHERINE MORALES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint. Denied for the following reasons. First, Plaintiffs failed to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1324. Second, Plaintiffs failed to comply with California Civil Code section 1714.10. That statute applies on its face, and Plaintiffs fail to show that any exception applies. Plaintiffs did not show that there is a reasonable probability that they will prevail in their cause of action against Defendant's attorneys. Third, many of the proposed amendments are improper and seek to add causes of action that were stricken by the Court in ruling on the earlier demurrer and motion to strike. Fourth, there is obvious prejudice to Defendants. Granting the motion would require taking Defendant's motions for summary judgment off calendar and continuing the trial date. Additionally, it would create a conflict of interest between Defendant and Defendant's counsel such that Defendant would have to engage new counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND SARNO VS. SEARIVER MARITIME INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S First Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 11, 2013, Line 26, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SARNO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Granted in part, and denied in part. Defendant shall produce all responsive documents that do not contain Mr. Quinones' thoughts, impressions, observations and/or thoughts on the subject incident as they are attorney work product. Defendant shall produce a privilege log for all withheld documents. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC. et al VS. ERIN THOMSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 11, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANT SEILER EPSTEIN ZIEGLER & APPLEGATE LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. The Court has jurisdiction to hear this motion despite defendant's appeal of the preliminary injunction order. "An appeal from an injunction pendente lite does not affect the power of the court to hear and decide the case on the merits, even though some of the same issues as were involved in the injunction pendente lite were disposed of by the later judgment." Nomm v Nomm (1958) 164 Cal.App.2d 663, 664. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the first cause of action arises from petitioning activity and is subject to CCP 425.16. In addition, even if the burden shifted, plaintiffs have met their burden of showing facts which establish their probability of prevailing under Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro v. Schectman (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1279. The motion is denied as to the second cause of action. This cause of action concerns petitioning activity. See Bleavins v. Demarest (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1533. Hpwever, as to the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing. The Court does not weigh the evidence on an anti-SLAPP motion, and the inferences from the Coyle declaration (2) support the conclusion that SEZA has Plaintiffs' confidential information and has reviewed/used it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
